summary: This is an extremely brief post where Joel Spolsky shares that he was quoted in the French newspaper Le Monde. The quote, translated from French, says 'Bush will really end up being the first president elected thanks to a usability bug.' This references the 2000 US presidential election controversy involving the confusing butterfly ballot design in Palm Beach County, Florida. Joel frames the election outcome as fundamentally a UI design failure.
key_points: The 2000 US presidential election outcome was influenced by poor ballot design — a usability problem
Joel connects software UI design principles to real-world consequences far beyond technology
The butterfly ballot is characterized as a 'bug' rather than a political issue, reframing the controversy through a technologist's lens
money_quotes: Bush will really end up being the first president elected thanks to a 'usability bug'
key_insight: Bad UI design can have consequences far beyond software — even deciding a presidential election.
